objections.description:
The BA's OBJ-7 rebuttal (Edge Case 5 in iteration 3 spec) correctly resolves the /allow-grant sub-path: for a compound command with a /allow grant matching 'git push', _check_git_allowlist in _evaluate_commit returns True in current code because the pattern matches the full compound string by substring (pattern in command). M2's return after push does not change this outcome — _evaluate_commit would have exited early at line 455 anyway. This portion of OBJ-7 is conceded by QA.

However, the BA rebuttal does not address the second execution path through _evaluate_push: when CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 is set (by the /push slash command) and a valid push grant passes all branch/head/remote validations (lines 590-598), _evaluate_push returns normally WITHOUT going through _check_git_allowlist (which returned False at line 583 — no /allow grant). This is the normal /push flow.

For a compound command 'git push origin main; git commit -m X' (no /allow grant, CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1, valid push grant, no commit grant):

- CURRENT CODE (no M2 return): _evaluate_push completes normally at line 598. Execution falls through to line 667: _looks_like_git_merge check. Then line 669: _looks_like_git_commit check — True (GIT_COMMAND_RE at line 102 includes '; ' in match character class r'(?:^|[\s;&|()`])git'). _evaluate_commit IS called. _check_git_allowlist at line 455 returns False (no /allow grant). Commit grant check (lines 460-472) finds no grant. _block_default_deny_commit fires at line 474. BLOCKED.

- M2 CODE (with return after push): _evaluate_push completes normally. _evaluate_command hits the 'return' immediately after the push call. _evaluate_commit is NEVER called. The compound command's git commit portion runs without any hook check. ALLOWED.

This is a concrete behavioral difference. The same gap applies to _evaluate_merge: compound 'git push origin main; git merge feature' would also skip _evaluate_merge under M2 on the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path.

Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) confirmed this is a BLOCKER: 'Yes. Adding return after _evaluate_push would skip _evaluate_commit, so git push ...; git-commit would be allowed as one Bash command... This changes the security model from all protected git operations in the command are checked to successful push authorizes the whole compound command. That violates the requirement to keep bypassable operations identical.'
objections.location: docs/dev/ticket-20260518-094616.md Edge Case 5 (lines 228-242) and AC2 (lines 254-262, specifically the 'exactly one return statement inside the _looks_like_git_push branch' clause)
objections.proposed_fix:
Edge Case 5 must be extended to acknowledge the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path. BA must choose one of:

(a) MINIMAL FIX: Remove the 'return' after _evaluate_push from the M2 Technical Hints dispatcher. The push branch becomes a non-returning call (matching current sequential behavior). The central _check_git_allowlist before commit/merge dispatches still achieves M2's structural benefit: for non-push commands with an /allow grant, the central check fires and they return early without reaching per-function checks. Push remains excluded from the central check (correctly). This is the lowest-risk fix.

(b) STRONGEST FIX: Add compound-command splitting before dispatch (split on &&, ;, |, || and evaluate each subcommand independently). This matches pretool-bash-safety.sh V3 design but is the largest change.

(c) INLINE GUARD: After _evaluate_push returns, only apply the early return when no other protected matcher fires. Implementation: add a check 'if not (_looks_like_git_commit(command) or _looks_like_git_merge(command))' before the return.

Whichever path is chosen: (1) AC2's 'exactly one return statement inside the _looks_like_git_push branch' clause must be removed or narrowed; (2) a new AC must be added: 'GIVEN CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 and a valid push grant, WHEN the command is git push origin main; git commit -m x (no /allow grant, no commit grant), THEN the hook exits 2 with BLOCKED: agent git commit default-deny.'
objections.codex_confirmation: Confirmed BLOCKER by Codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh reasoning, session 019e3aa3-9a57-7bb0-99a9-60c1284ed7f3): 'M2 introduces a commit-protection bypass on the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path. With a valid /push env+grant and no /allow grant, current code continues after _evaluate_push and reaches _evaluate_commit. Adding return would skip _evaluate_commit so the compound command commit would be allowed. Security regression: changes model from all protected operations checked to successful push authorizes whole compound command. Violates requirement to keep bypassable operations identical.'

resolved_objections.resolution: RESOLVED. The broken first dispatcher implementation and the 'Actually, reconsider' narrative have been removed. Technical Hints now begins with 'Rejected approach (not used)' and presents only the correct push-first dispatcher structure. Verified: only one dispatcher implementation is present in the updated spec.

SUPERSEDED BY OBJ-7 (iteration 2), which itself is partially resolved and partially superseded by OBJ-8 (this iteration). The compound-command security analysis in Edge Case 5 was added per OBJ-2's request, but remains incomplete on the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path (see OBJ-8).
RESOLVED. M4 explicitly includes replacing _DO_IS_SUB with a reference to $IS_SUBAGENT. AC6 includes the criterion. Technical Hints M4 section includes exact replacement code. Verified.

RESOLVED. 'sole consume mechanism' is replaced throughout with 'sole PreToolUse allowlist match/approval call site in pretool-bash-safety.sh; actual grant deletion remains deferred to posttool-allowlist-consume.py (PostToolUse)'. Verified in both M3 requirement text and AC4 THEN clause.

RESOLVED. M3 now includes removal of the stale comment at lines 690-696. M4 now includes update of the stale comment at lines 124-131. The comment at lines 1064-1068 is addressed by noting it relocates with the IS_SUBAGENT variable assignment. Verified.

RESOLVED. The allow-6 analysis now correctly states posttool-allowlist-consume.py implements its own independent grant-read and consume logic at lines 38-104, does NOT use hooks/lib/allowlist.py, imports only lib.subagent for subagent detection, and is therefore a FOURTH independent implementation. Verified against actual file.

PARTIALLY RESOLVED. BA's rebuttal is correct and well-evidenced for the /allow grant path: _check_git_allowlist uses full-string substring matching (confirmed by source read, lines 141-177 of pretool-git-privilege-guard.py), so a pattern like 'git push' matches the full compound string in _evaluate_commit just as it does in _evaluate_push. M2's return does not change the security outcome on this specific path. QA concedes OBJ-7's original analysis was wrong about the /allow path.

HOWEVER: OBJ-7 is superseded by OBJ-8, which identifies the separate CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE + push-grant path (the normal /push flow, not /allow) where M2's return IS a genuine security regression. The BA's rebuttal, while correct for the /allow path, does not address this path. OBJ-8 blocks the spec.

observations: BA's full-string matching analysis for _check_git_allowlist is verified correct by source reading: lines 141-177 of pretool-git-privilege-guard.py confirm 'pattern in command' (literal) and 're.search(pattern, command)' (regex) against the full compound string with no splitting.

GIT_COMMAND_RE at line 102: r'(?:^|[\s;&|()`])git' — the character class includes ';' and '&', confirming compound commands are explicitly in scope for this hook's matchers. _looks_like_git_commit IS True for 'git push origin main; git commit -m x'.

_evaluate_push has two success paths: (1) _check_git_allowlist returns True (/allow grant path, lines 583-584); (2) CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 + valid push grant validates (lines 586-598, normal /push path). BA's OBJ-7 rebuttal addresses only path (1). Path (2) is the gap identified in OBJ-8.

Codex independently confirmed OBJ-8 is a BLOCKER (gpt-5.5 xhigh reasoning). Codex also noted the merge path: compound 'git push ...; git merge ...' would similarly skip _evaluate_merge under M2 on the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path.
OBJ-1 through OBJ-6 remain cleanly resolved. No regressions to prior resolutions detected.
M1, M3, M4 requirements and their ACs are well-specified and testable. Only M2 (and specifically AC2's 'exactly one return statement' clause) is blocked.

The minimal fix for OBJ-8 is lower-risk than the compound-splitting approaches: simply remove the 'return' from the push branch in M2's Technical Hints. The central _check_git_allowlist before commit/merge dispatches still achieves M2's structural benefit (single allowlist check for non-push ops) without requiring an early exit.
qa.summary: Iteration 3 review: OBJ-1 through OBJ-6 are cleanly resolved. OBJ-7 is partially resolved — BA's rebuttal is correct for the /allow grant sub-path (QA concedes this portion of OBJ-7). However, OBJ-7 is superseded by OBJ-8: the BA's rebuttal does not address the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE + push-grant path (normal /push flow), where M2's 'return' after push dispatch IS a genuine security regression. Currently, the sequential dispatcher calls _evaluate_commit (which default-denies ungranted commits). M2 would skip it entirely for any compound command where push succeeds via the env/grant mechanism. Codex confirms this is a BLOCKER. The minimal fix is to remove the 'return' from the push branch in the M2 Technical Hints — the central _check_git_allowlist before commit/merge dispatches still achieves M2's structural goal without the early exit.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex verdict: OBJ-8 is a BLOCKER. Finding 1 (PROPOSED_FIX / BLOCKER): M2 introduces commit-protection bypass on CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path — QA analysis is correct, real new bypass, security regression violating 'keep bypassable operations identical'. Proposed fix: preserve all-matching sequential evaluation OR split/parse requiring independent authorization per protected operation. Finding 2 (OBSERVATION_ONLY / HIGH): BA rebuttal covers only /allow path, not normal /push grant path — cannot generalize. Finding 3 (PROPOSED_FIX / HIGH): same early-return also skips _evaluate_merge for compound 'git push; git merge' on CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path; regression tests needed.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: OBJ-8 description includes explicit confirmation that Codex agrees CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path analysis is correct. Codex's finding 3 (merge path gap) incorporated into OBJ-8 description and observations. Codex's observation-only finding (BA rebuttal scope) incorporated into OBJ-7 resolution note. Codex's proposed fix options (no-return vs splitting) incorporated into OBJ-8 proposed_fix. Codex's required test scenarios incorporated into OBJ-8 proposed_fix's new AC requirement.
